title: Calculate the sum of digits of a number

content.problemStatement:
In this task, you are required to implement a function that calculates the sum of digits of a given number using the reduce() function from the functools module in Python. The reduce() function applies a rolling computation to sequential pairs of values in a list.

For example, if the input number is 12345, the function should return 15 (1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 = 15).

Your task is to complete the sum_of_digits() function using the reduce() function to perform the calculation.
constraints: Each N has at most 1000 decimal digits
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10⁴), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N. N may be negative and can have up to 1000 decimal digits.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the sum of the decimal digits of |N| (the absolute value of N).

hidden.label: edge case with leading zeros and negative sign

solution.approach: For every test case we treat the number as a string, ignore a possible leading minus sign and sum all characters that are digits. In Python the required reduce() from functools is used: we map each character to its integer value and reduce with addition, starting from 0. All other languages perform the same digit‑by‑digit addition using a simple loop. The algorithm is linear in the number of digits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T · L) where L is the number of digits of a test case (at most 1000).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) for storing the string representation of each number.
